name: Trim The Grouped-Row 128x128 Sibling Export Scratch To The PTX-Style Single Stage

summary.name: Trim The Grouped-Row 128x128 Sibling Export Scratch To The PTX-Style Single Stage
summary.hypothesis: Round 9 turned the grouped-row non-PTX 128x128 sibling from a historical escape hatch into a directly measured live branch: switching the default hot-band dispatch onto that sibling measured 24.18073654 ms, which is only 0.00008011 ms slower than the accepted-base run at 24.18065643 ms and therefore effectively PASS_FLAT on the active search anchor. The NCU headline signature also stayed in the same band instead of collapsing: active warps held at 16.62, long-scoreboard stayed at 7.26, and barrier only moved to 5.48. That means the branch validation step is done. The cleanest next move is the historically positive follow-on inside that family: replace the sibling's heavier generic export scratch lifetime with the PTX-style single-stage per-warp scratch path that previously improved the sibling branch from 24.449024 ms to 24.422464 ms. Now that the sibling route is live in the current tree, this bounded export-side trim is the highest-upside next step without reopening a broader family pivot.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Shared export and writeback overhead inside the grouped-row non-PTX 128x128 sibling, especially the sibling branch's heavier export scratch lifetime after the hot-band dispatch has already moved onto that family.
summary.risk: Medium. The family is bounded and historically positive, but it still leaves the absolute best-known PTX run behind unless the sibling export trim converts the newly validated flat branch into a real win.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164352 ms best-known PTX run
end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.180656 ms accepted-base run
shared-memory footprint or allocation on the sibling path
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: Round 9 removed the main coherence objection to this family by proving the sibling dispatch itself is flat on the accepted surface rather than a real regression.
This is the highest-priority open frontier family after the live-queue rehydration and it now lines up with the latest measured evidence instead of only historical runs.
It ranks above the PTX-local fallbacks because it attacks the most concrete residual cost on the only newly validated off-branch surface.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the sibling export-scratch trim cannot beat the accepted-base 24.180656 ms band or if it improves sibling-local counters without moving runtime enough to threaten the 24.164352 ms best-known PTX run.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
